# HAL 9000

> A local, cross-platform, multimodal AI agent that sees, hears, thinks, speaks, and acts on your machine.

HAL 9000 is an open-source AI agent that runs entirely on your computer. It captures webcam video, listens to your voice, processes requests through an LLM (GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini, or Ollama), speaks responses aloud, and executes 40 OS-level tools via function calling. It integrates with Claude Code as an MCP server for bidirectional AI collaboration.

## Key Features

- Vision: Webcam capture with real-time frame analysis
- Hearing: Microphone input with VAD + Whisper STT (cloud API or local faster-whisper)
- Brain: Multi-provider LLM with function calling (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama)
- Voice: 3 TTS providers (Edge TTS free, ElevenLabs paid, XTTS local voice cloning)
- Tools: 40 OS-level tools — shell, files, apps, web search, clipboard, screenshots, notifications
- Co-Work: Background task runner, multi-agent orchestration, artifact workspace, typed memory with session handoff
- MCP Server: 20 tools exposed to Claude Code/Desktop for cross-agent collaboration
- Cross-Platform: macOS, Windows, Linux — auto-detects OS, uses native system commands
- Free Mode: FREE_MODE=true uses Ollama + faster-whisper + Edge TTS — zero API keys needed

## Architecture

Vision + Hearing + Chat UI feed into Brain (LLM + function calling). Brain calls tools, generates voice output. Backed by typed persistent memory (facts, decisions, preferences, session summaries), background task runner, multi-agent orchestrator, and artifact store.

## Installation

```
git clone https://github.com/shandar/HAL9000.git
cd HAL9000
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
echo "FREE_MODE=true" > .env  # or add API keys
python server.py
```

Open http://localhost:9000 and click Activate.
